html:
Gunhild is an Old Norse female name composed of the words "gunnr" and "hild," both meaning battle. The name has been used in Sweden since at least the 10th century.

The name appears on runestones, for example, on U 652 in Kumla, Övergrans parish, Uppland: "Gunhild had these markings made for Tjälve, her son, and for herself. Arbjörn carved this stone."

The name was relatively popular in the 1940s and 1950s, but its usage has significantly decreased since then. The average age of people named Gunhild is 79 years (as of 2019).

A Latin variant of the name is Gunilla.

As of December 31, 2014, there were a total of 11,537 women registered in Sweden with the name Gunhild, of whom 4,037 used it as their given name. The corresponding figures for Gunhilda were 17 and 1, respectively.

Name day in Sweden: January 30th. In the Swedish-speaking Finnish calendar of names, Gunhild's name day is November 1st, since 1950.

== People named Gunhild ==

  • Gunhild, Swedish queen consort to King Erik Segersäll and later Sven Tveskägg
  • Gunhild, Swedish queen consort to King Anund Jakob, of the Norwegian jarls' lineage
  • Gunhild, Norwegian queen consort to King Eric Bloodaxe
  • Gunhild of Denmark, German queen consort to King Henry III
  • Gunhild Anundsdotter, Danish queen, born as a Swedish princess
  • Gunhild Bolander, Swedish politician (Center Party)
  • Gunhild Carling, Swedish musician
  • Gunhild Haraldsdatter, Danish princess
  • Gunhild Hoffmeister, East German track and field athlete
  • Gunhild Kyle, Swedish historian
  • Gunhild Larking, Swedish track and field athlete
  • Gunhild Palmqvist, Swedish politician (Free People's Party)
  • Gunhild Robertson, Swedish actress
  • Gunhild Rosén, Swedish ballerina and ballet mistress for the Royal Ballet
  • Gunhild Schytte-Jacobsen, Norwegian actress
  • Gunhild Sehlin, Swedish children's book author
  • Gunhild Stordalen, Norwegian physician, philanthropist, and environmental activist
  • Gunhild Tegen, Swedish author
  • Gunhild Wallin, Swedish politician (Center Party)

== Sources ==

How certain are we about this?

For people living in the Middle Ages, there are often contemporary written sources available (letters, chronicles, church documents), so the main aspects of their lives are usually well-documented. However, these sources were primarily written by the Church and those in power, and they have been preserved unevenly, which can distort and limit our understanding.
tldr: Gunhild is an old Swedish name that means "battle" and has been used in Sweden since the 10th century.
lattlast:
The name Gunhild is composed of two words, both meaning "battle." It's a very old name that has appeared on runestones in Sweden for over a thousand years.

During the 1940s and 1950s, the name was quite common for children, but today it is not used as frequently. On average, people named Gunhild in Sweden are 79 years old.

There are many well-known individuals throughout history and in modern times who bear the name Gunhild. These include queens, politicians, musicians, and athletes.
